issues.title: SupabaseClient type alias duplicated in 3 app-level shared domains
issues.description: The type alias `export type SupabaseClient = ReturnType<typeof createBrowserSupabaseClient>` is defined identically in three separate apps. Each app imports from 'api/supabase' and re-declares the same type alias.
issues.recommendation: Export `SupabaseClient` from `packages/api/src` (or `packages/shared`) so all apps can import from a single source. The type alias is purely derived from `createBrowserSupabaseClient` and carries no app-specific knowledge.
issues.rule: DRY - Type Duplication / Single Source of Truth

issues.title: escapeLikePattern utility function duplicated in admin and studio
issues.description: The function `escapeLikePattern` (escaping SQL LIKE wildcards `%_\`) has identical implementations in two different infrastructure files in different apps. The studio version is exported; the admin version is a private local function.
issues.recommendation: Extract `escapeLikePattern` to `packages/shared/src/utils/` and export it from there. Both apps already depend on the `shared` package. This is a pure utility with no app-specific logic.
issues.rule: DRY - Rule of Three / Duplicate Code Blocks

issues.title: Seller profile display-name resolution logic duplicated across store and payments
issues.description: Two separate `useSellerProfiles` hooks and an infrastructure-level `fetchSellerProfiles` function all query the same Supabase `user_profiles` table with the same `select('id, display_name, email').in('id', ids)` pattern. The store hook inlines the query and uses `email.split('@')[0]` as fallback; the payments version delegates to `fetchSellerProfiles` which uses a `FALLBACK_SELLER_NAME` constant. Both resolve the same business need (seller display name map).
issues.recommendation: The `fetchUserDisplayNames` function in `apps/payments/src/shared/infrastructure/fetchUserDisplayNames.ts` already abstracts the user_profiles query. The store's `useSellerProfiles` should be refactored to use a similar extracted query function. Longer-term, if both store and payments display seller names, a shared infrastructure utility in `packages/shared` could eliminate the cross-app duplication.

issues.title: Product type alias duplicated in store and studio feature domains
issues.description: `export type Product = Tables<'products'>` is defined identically in both `apps/store/src/features/products/domain/types.ts` and `apps/studio/src/features/products/domain/types.ts`. Both apps import from `api/supabase/types` and re-declare the same derived type.
issues.recommendation: The `Product` type could be exported from `packages/api/src` (alongside the Supabase generated types) or from `packages/shared/src/types`. The store's version adds a helper function `isProductAvailable` and re-exports shared types, so it may need to remain as a thin wrapper — but the raw `Tables<'products'>` alias should come from a single source.

issues.title: Time-conversion constants defined in 3 separate feature domains
issues.description:
`MS_PER_SECOND = 1000`, `SECONDS_PER_MINUTE = 60`, `MINUTES_PER_HOUR = 60` are defined in:
- `apps/payments/src/features/checkout/domain/constants.ts`
- `apps/admin/src/features/users/domain/constants.ts`
- `apps/store/src/features/cart/application/cartCookiePersistence.ts` (private inline constants with same values)

These are universal mathematical constants with no business meaning.
issues.recommendation: Export these time-conversion constants from `packages/shared/src/utils/time.ts` (or a `constants.ts`). All three apps depend on `shared` already. This is a low-priority cleanup since the values are obvious, but having a single source prevents future drift.
issues.rule: DRY - Rule of Three / Magic Values

issues.title: Nearly-identical vitest.config.ts files across all apps
issues.description:
All 5 production app vitest configs (`store`, `admin`, `payments`, `auth`, `studio`) share the same structure: identical plugin setup, same test/coverage/timeout settings, and the same coverage exclude patterns. The only per-app differences are minor exclusion additions. The root already has a shared `vitest.aliases.ts` helper but no shared base config.

Note: Two apps (`payments` and `auth`) also contain the duplicate string `.next/**` in their coverage exclude arrays.
issues.recommendation: Extract a `createVitestConfig(options)` factory (similar to `createVitestAliases`) to the repo root (`vitest.base.ts`). Each app's config calls the factory and passes only the app-specific overrides (e.g., additional coverage excludes). This is a config-level KISS improvement — not urgent, but prevents config drift. Also fix the duplicate `.next/**` entry in `payments` and `auth` vitest configs.

patterns.name: fetchUserDisplayNames abstraction (positive)
patterns.description: The payments app correctly extracted `fetchUserDisplayNames` into `apps/payments/src/shared/infrastructure/` as a reusable function used by both `orderQueries` and `receivedOrderQueries`. This is the right pattern and should be the model for the store's `useSellerProfiles` inline logic.

patterns.name: Vitest aliases factory (positive)
patterns.description: The repo root `vitest.aliases.ts` already extracts the shared alias configuration into a factory function. This pattern should be extended to the full vitest base config.
